evidence: Capital expenditures consisted primarily of investments in software...including those driven by AI...We anticipate capital expenditures in 2026 to be approximately $75 mi[llion]
With approximately 800 technologists across product, data, engineering, and AI, we continue to make smart, talent-focused investments globally in this critical area

We expect to incur restructuring charges of $50 million to $75 million in total over the duration of the 2025 Restructuring Program primarily related to severance...and integration of automation and AI-driven solutions
Our fleet of more than 30 AI agents is integrated with Navisphere, our global, multimodal transportation management system

Using large language models, generative AI, and agentic AI, we have created proprietary technology that automates steps across the lifecycle of a shipment: from giving customers a price quote, to processing orders, to setting appointments
Our enhancements to our dynamic costing and pricing models are key contributors to expanding our operating margins and growing volume and market share

The use or offering of AI technologies may result in new or expanded risks and liabilities, including enhanced government or regulatory scrutiny, litigation, privacy and compliance issues, ethical concerns, confidentiality, reputational harm, and security risks

If we fail to maintain the pace, scale, or quality of automation and AI adoption, we may be unable to achieve our strategic goals for operational efficiency and digital transformation...could increase our cost to serve customers

The cost of complying with laws and regulations governing AI could be significant and would increase our operating expenses, which could adversely affect our business, financial condition, and results of operations

AI narrative concreteness scored 4 rather than 5 because quantified business results (e.g., shipments per person per day improvement magnitude) are referenced as a metric category but no specific numbers are disclosed in the excerpts
